Applied physics solves everyday problems—from free fall to home electrical systems. Formulas have been validated through centuries of experiments.

## How It's Calculated

Calculate cylinder volume and surface area instantly. This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to any unreasonable values.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, or legal), consult with a qualified professional. Our data is current as of 2026 and regularly reviewed.

faq.q: How do you calculate the volume of a cylinder?
faq.a: Use the formula V = πr²h, where r is the radius and h is the height. This calculator applies it instantly with your values.

faq.q: What is the cylinder surface area formula?
faq.a: Total surface area is S = 2πr(r+h), which includes both circular bases plus the lateral (curved) surface.

faq.q: How do I calculate the lateral surface area?
faq.a: Lateral surface area (without the circular ends) is S_lat = 2πrh. Use this for applications like pipes or cylinders without lids.

faq.q: Can I use this to calculate tank capacity in liters?
faq.a: Yes. The calculator gives volume in cubic centimeters. Convert to liters by dividing by 1,000 (1 L = 1,000 cm³).

faq.q: How do you calculate the volume of a hollow cylinder?
faq.a: For a hollow cylinder, use V = π(R²−r²)h, where R is the outer radius and r is the inner radius.
Cylinders appear everywhere—grain silos, water pipes, metal cans, hydraulic cylinders, and industrial tanks. This calculator helps with all of them.

faq.q: Are the results accurate for real-world use?
faq.a: The formulas are mathematically precise. However, for critical decisions (engineering projects, medical dosing), verify with a professional engineer or specialist.
